content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

March 7, 2017

Steven T. Mattas
Meyers Nave
575 Market Street, Suite 2080
San Francisco, CA 94105

Re:    Your Request for Advice
       Our File No. W-16-210

Dear Mr. Mattas:

       This letter is written notification that we have withdrawn the above-captioned request for
advice on behalf of El Cajon Oversight Board Member Michelle Nguyen regarding the provisions
of the Political Reform Act¹ and Government Code section 1090. As of this date, we have not
received additional information we requested on February 3, 2017, which is necessary for our
analysis.

       Your request for advice has therefore been withdrawn. If you are able to obtain the
requested information in the future, please submit another advice request.

       Should you have any questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

By:    Emelyn C. Rodriguez
       Senior Counsel, Legal Division

ECR:cc

¹ Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997
of the California Code of Regulations.

Your request for advice has therefore been withdrawn. If you are able to obtain the 
requested information in the future, please submit another advice request.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether El Cajon Oversight Board Member Michelle Nguyen's conduct complies with the Political Reform Act and Government Code section 1090.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC withdrew the request for advice due to the requestor's failure to provide additional information requested on February 3, 2017, which was necessary for analysis.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: This is a withdrawal letter, not a standard advice letter with Q/C sections. The question and conclusion are inferred from the document's subject matter rather than explicit Q/A sections. File number W-16-210 indicates a withdrawn matter.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether El Cajon Oversight Board Member Michelle Nguyen's conduct complies with the Political Reform Act and Government Code section 1090.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC withdrew the request for advice due to the requestor's failure to provide additional information requested on February 3, 2017, which was necessary for analysis.
